heading: Saving money and reducing tragedies through prevention (SMART Prevention)
label: 12463 Saving money and reducing tragedies through prevention (SMART Prevention)

children.heading: Grants authorized
children.label: a Grants authorized
children.text: The Attorney General, in consultation with the Secretary of Health and Human Services and the Secretary of Education, is authorized to award grants for the purpose of preventing domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, and stalking by focusing on men and youth as leaders and influencers of social norms.

children.heading: Use of funds
children.label: b Use of funds
children.text: Funds provided under this section may be used to develop, maintain or enhance programs that work with men and youth to prevent domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, and stalking by helping men and youth to serve as role models and social influencers of other men and youth at the individual, school, community or statewide levels.

a victim service provider, community-based organization, tribe or tribal organization, or other non-profit, nongovernmental organization that has a history of effective work preventing domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, or stalking and expertise in the specific area for which they are applying for funds; or

children.label: A
children.text: A public, charter, tribal, or nationally accredited private middle or high school, a school administered by the Department of Defense under or , a group of schools, or a school district.section 2164 of title 10section 921 of title 20

children.label: B
children.text: A local community-based organization, population-specific organization, or faith-based organization that has established expertise in providing services to youth.

children.label: C
children.text: A community-based organization, population-specific organization, university or health care clinic, faith-based organization, or other non-profit, nongovernmental organization with a demonstrated history of effective work addressing the needs of children exposed to domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, or stalking.

children.label: D
children.text: A nonprofit, nongovernmental entity providing services for runaway or homeless youth affected by domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, or stalking.

children.label: E
children.text: Healthcare entities eligible for reimbursement under title XVIII of the Social Security Act [ et seq.], including providers that target the special needs of children and youth.42 U.S.C. 1395

children.label: F
children.text: Any other agencies, population-specific organizations, or nonprofit, nongovernmental organizations with the capacity to provide necessary expertise to meet the goals of the program; or

a public, charter, tribal, or nationally accredited private middle or high school, a school administered by the Department of Defense under or , a group of schools, a school district, or an institution of higher education.section 2164 of title 10section 921 of title 20

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Applicants for grants under this section shall prepare and submit to the Director an application at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Director may require that demonstrates the capacity of the applicant and partnering organizations to undertake the project.

children.label: A
children.text: include appropriate referral systems to direct any victim identified during program activities to highly qualified follow-up care;

children.label: B
children.text: protect the confidentiality and privacy of adult and youth victim information, particularly in the context of parental or third party involvement and consent, mandatory reporting duties, and working with other service providers;

children.label: C
children.text: ensure that all individuals providing prevention programming through a program funded under this section have completed or will complete sufficient training in connection with domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault or stalking; and

children.label: D
children.text: document how prevention programs are coordinated with service programs in the community.

children.label: A
children.text: include outcome-based evaluation;

children.label: B
children.text: identify any other community, school, or State-based efforts that are working on domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, or stalking prevention and explain how the grantee or partnership will add value, coordinate with other programs, and not duplicate existing efforts; and

children.label: C
children.text: include a focus on the unmet needs of underserved populations.

children.heading: Definitions and grant conditions
children.label: e Definitions and grant conditions
children.text: In this section, the definitions and grant conditions provided for in shall apply.section 12291 of this title

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: f Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section, $20,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2023 through 2027. Amounts appropriated under this section may only be used for programs and activities described under this section.
